---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Paulina Del Mundo — Evidence synthesis methods, applied to clinical AI"
description: "Physician + MPH (Hopkins epi/biostats) writing on risk of bias, evidence synthesis, and what clinical AI is actually synthesizing."
page-layout: article
toc: false
listing:
  - id: recent-writing
    contents: "writing/2*.qmd"
    sort: "date desc"
    max-items: 3
    type: default
    fields: [date, title, description, categories]
    field-display-names:
      categories: "Tags"
    feed: false
    categories: false
    sort-ui: false
    filter-ui: false
---

::: {.hero}

<h1 class="hero-title">Evidence synthesis methods,<br>applied to clinical AI.</h1>

<p class="hero-lede">I'm Paulina — physician, MPH epi/biostats from Hopkins, clinical data scientist working with EHR and claims at scale. I write about what clinical AI is actually synthesizing, and whether the evidence underneath it holds up.</p>

::: {.hero-cta}
[Read the latest →](writing/) &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; [Subscribe](#subscribe)
:::

::: {.trust-strip}
[MD]{} [MPH Hopkins]{} [24.6M+ encounters analyzed]{} [4 case studies]{}
:::

:::

::: {.section-head}
[Recent writing]{.section-label}
[All writing →](writing/){.section-more}
:::

::: {#recent-writing}
:::

::: {.section-head}
[Selected work]{.section-label}
[All projects →](projects/){.section-more}
:::

::: {.work-grid}

::: {.work-card}
[04]{.work-num}

### Medicare Part D insulin cap — a difference-in-differences read

What did the \$35 insulin cap actually shift in claims data, and how confident should we be in the answer?

[View analysis →](projects/04-medicare-part-d-insulin-cap/analysis.html){.work-link}
:::

::: {.work-card}
[02]{.work-num}

### Medicaid outlier detection

Spotting anomalous billing patterns in Medicaid claims without flagging the wrong thing — a method comparison.

[View analysis →](projects/02-medicaid-outliers/analysis.html){.work-link}
:::

:::

::: {.subscribe-block #subscribe}
### Subscribe

One essay every two weeks on evidence synthesis methods in clinical AI. Risk of bias, study teardowns, framework drafts. No spam, easy unsubscribe.

<form class="subscribe-form" action="https://buttondown.email/api/emails/embed-subscribe/paulinadelmundomd" method="post" target="popupwindow" onsubmit="window.open('https://buttondown.email/paulinadelmundomd', 'popupwindow')">
  <input type="email" name="email" placeholder="your@email.com" required />
  <button type="submit">Subscribe</button>
</form>
:::
